entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated brain uses a self-wiring knowledge graph that performs entity linking without LLM calls to maintain high efficiency. It automates the ingestion of diverse data sources like emails and voice calls, offering MCP server integration for tools like Claude Code.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements biomimetic data structures to facilitate long-term learning through dedicated retain, recall, and reflect workflows. It is designed for deep mental model organization and integrates seamlessly with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness utilizes a multi-agent loop of specialized roles, such as architects and curators, to accumulate validated knowledge. It improves performance on complex tasks through verifier-driven checkpoints and browser-integrated investigations.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Designed for lossless context management, this plugin uses a hierarchical DAG-based engine to compact history without deleting data. It relies on immutable-first SQLite storage and provides specific agent tools for searching and expanding compacted summaries.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This backend provides a private, self-hosted solution for cross-session memory persistence. It supports any OpenAI-compatible provider and includes an automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis environments.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that utilizes a unified SQLite vault to ensure no cloud dependencies. It features hybrid retrieval via vector search, BM25, and graph traversal to automate decision capture and handoff generation.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur provides a shared memory layer for multi-agent systems using an open YAML engram format for local storage. It enables zero-cost retrieval through hybrid search and automates memory injection for Hermes and MCP tools.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: This reflective memory system indexes codebases and media using automatic summarization and OCR. It creates navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes via edge tags and offers both MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency system optimized for sub-millisecond local retrieval using SQLite-vec and FTS5. It employs a three-tier BEAM architecture to manage working, episodic, and scratchpad memory tiers.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system uses RAG to prefetch context by monitoring agent session logs. It is a local-first solution utilizing SQLite-vec and GGUF models, featuring an MCP-native server for automated configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry standards like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks across 26 domains, including malware analysis and cloud security, using the agentskills.io integration standard.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a comprehensive 109-entry word replacement table. It features a two-pass audit system compatible with Hermes Agent to ensure natural-sounding, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides Hermes with modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It utilizes a dedicated CLI for easy installation and is fully compatible with the agentskills.io open standard.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills specification within the Pydantic AI framework. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate six types of professional diagrams, including UML and ML architectures, directly from natural language. It includes a five-round self-check safety valve to iteratively refine visual accuracy and grid alignment.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: Designed for blockchain interoperability, this library enables Hermes to perform cross-chain sends via CCIP and monitor multi-chain data feeds. It follows the agentskills.io spec to provide standardized smart contract interaction capabilities.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill implements literate programming by allowing Hermes to weave runnable code and prose into unified markdown documents. It features a 'tangler' to extract source files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides Hermes with cross-instance shared memory and a pipeline for training its own replacement models. It extracts decision-quality data from active workflows to facilitate fine-tuning and performance evaluation.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: This registry offers a Python SDK and CLI for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It uniquely supports skill monetization through Stripe and the TON blockchain while handling automated dependency resolution.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills integrate FLUX image generation capabilities into Hermes with precise JSON-structured prompting and hex color support. The package includes standardized guidelines and API patterns for robust error handling and webhook integration.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: A meta-skill plugin that monitors Hermes' active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing the agent to expand its own capabilities via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a local-first harness that manages long-running missions through structured planning, milestones, and JSON snapshots. It enables seamless handoffs between agent runs by generating detailed markdown briefs and validation assertions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill empowers Hermes to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously with real-time progress tracking. It ensures operational safety by providing built-in checkpoints and rollback procedures for recovery from execution errors.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It maintains persistent server connections via a socket-based daemon and supports hot-reloading for dynamic configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: This utility converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It provides a verifiable audit trail with line-level provenance to ensure the agent's actions are grounded in source material.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: A community-driven hub for browsing and installing Hermes skills with built-in security scanning for prompt injection. It features a decentralized trust system with proof-of-use reviews and supports micropayments for premium skill developers.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, allowing C# developers to build interoperable skills for Hermes. It implements progressive disclosure to save tokens and supports automatic discovery from standard agent directories.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This collection provides daily-updated Nix packages for a wide range of AI coding tools, including Hermes and Claude Code. It is ideal for users who need a unified proxy API and multi-agent orchestrators within a reproducible Nix-based environment.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: This security-focused deployment uses NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking to provide hardware-enforced sandboxing for Hermes. It includes over 40 integrated tools and pre-configured security policies that range from strict inference to permissive system access.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes configurations, documents, and systemd services with a focus on reproducibility. It features secure secrets handling via sops-nix and supports a hybrid skill architecture for both managed and interactive installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this no-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 tools without requiring Docker or administrative rights. It integrates local model management, GPU monitoring, and a workflow engine for scheduled automation directly on the desktop.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project offers a streamlined Docker-based sandbox that includes both the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to target specific Hermes versions using build arguments.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production environments, this headless deployment configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, allowing the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs and integrate with Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This Docker Compose stack integrates with Portainer to deploy Hermes alongside a ttyd web terminal for browser-based interaction. It simplifies the deployment lifecycle by using GitHub Actions and GHCR for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require cost monitoring and a human-in-the-loop 'Aegis' system for manual task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw provides a self-hosted runtime for building autonomous agent swarms with native support for Hermes and 22 other model providers. It stands out by offering MCP-native skills, persistent memory, and scheduled heartbeats to maintain long-running agent delegations.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This project implements a meta-optimization harness that uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the code used for evaluating Hermes. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, vie for GitHub stars. The framework orchestrates nine specialized agents and visualizes their real-time tasks, code generation, and scoreboard metrics on a live dashboard.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This framework coordinates a massive fleet of 17 specialized agents across distinct development phases from research to infrastructure. It ensures high-fidelity outputs by enforcing mandatory quality gates for code reviews and testing across multiple LLM backends.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron automates an eight-phase software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It differentiates itself by using graph-diff phase gates to enforce architectural integrity throughout the autonomous coding process.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation tool routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution in the parent agent. It prioritizes operational safety by implementing isolated workflows with configurable timeouts and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides real-time token tracking and cost visualization for Hermes Agent and other AI coding assistants. Its native TUI allows developers to monitor local data usage across multiple models and providers simultaneously.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI using YAML-based themes and ASCII art. It supports partial skin inheritance and includes a variety of community presets ranging from retro DOS aesthetics to sci-fi themes.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A native port of the Hermes Agent brain and its 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. It eliminates the need for external processes or OAuth while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM inference and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: This AI-native browser enables autonomous agents to navigate the web via a structured MCP interface. It provides human-visible auditing through annotated checkpoints and persistent sessions, ensuring developers can supervise agent actions in real-time.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: Ankh.md is a framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It allows for seamless switching between global and local agents, each with its own unique set of memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: This static linter identifies structural bugs in agent configurations and system prompts without requiring LLM calls or API keys. It uses seven specialized detectors to provide automated quality gating and HERM v1.1 scoring within CI/CD pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations to Hermes-compatible YAML. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, and credentials to ensure a smooth platform switch.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: A rapid deployment script that uses Docker to stand up a full Hermes Agent stack including LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and automated routing for local GPU inference and free model usage.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A zero-build web interface that provides full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI. It utilizes a three-panel layout to streamline chat, file browsing, and session management across desktop and mobile browsers.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity and operational oversight. It features unified configuration for channels like Telegram and Discord, alongside real-time usage analytics for token consumption and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A security-hardened native web workspace featuring execution approval prompts and auth middleware. It offers deep orchestration tools, including a dedicated skills manager and a comprehensive memory browser.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing multiple agent profiles from a single dashboard. It combines a browser-based terminal and system metrics with detailed token analytics categorized by model and platform.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that connects directly via SSH without requiring a remote helper service. It provides a professional multi-tabbed terminal with live theme controls for managing sessions and cron jobs.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA specifically designed as a Telegram Mini App. It enables background agent spawning with live output monitoring and integrated system health gauges for hardware resources.
entries.Web3CZ/Web3Hermes: A localized Chinese version of the Hermes WebUI tailored for accessibility within the Chinese community. It includes a workspace file browser for direct editing and tool call visualization within session management.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A desktop companion app that simplifies the onboarding process through guided installation and dependency resolution. It supports a wide array of LLM providers and local endpoints alongside integrated messaging gateways.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A specialized monitoring dashboard focused on process oversight and configuration. It features FTS5 search for conversation history inspection and real-time tracking of cost distribution.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that integrates a marketplace for MCP servers, plugins, and skills.sh, utilizing isolated profile management to separate sessions, memory, and API keys.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A glassmorphic React 18 interface built on a single-file architecture. It features a multi-tab terminal that supports simultaneous Shell, Hermes, and Claude Code workflows.
